observations.label: Find the work input
observations.demand: The user must find the input surface before they can repair an unclear request.

observations.label: Choose deterministic adapter
observations.demand: The tester chooses a deterministic adapter so the recovery loop can be inspected repeatably.

observations.label: Submit an underspecified request
observations.demand: The user expresses intent but omits the object, authority, and source context required for safe completion.

observations.label: Receive first answer
observations.demand: The user must judge whether the answer resolves the missing context or requires a repair turn.

observations.label: Repair the request with added context
observations.demand: The user must reconstruct missing details, name uncertainty, and avoid accepting accountability they cannot verify.

observations.label: Repair the request with added context
Receive second answer
observations.demand: The user must decide whether the repaired answer has enough authority and evidence to close the loop.

observations.label: Open process trace after repair
observations.demand: The user switches into audit mode to check whether the repair turn changed the process state.

findings.label: Recovery recursion finding
findings.risk: Repair turns can shift hidden work onto the human: remembering context, clarifying authority, and checking whether the AI incorporated the correction.
findings.mitigation: Make missing fields, authority gaps, and incorporated corrections explicit before the user is asked to close the loop.
